rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT RAINBOW WATERPROOFING & RESTORATION CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Cross-defendant Rainbow Waterproofing & Restoration Co.'s motion for summary judgment and its alternative motion for summary adjudication of the cross-complaint is granted as to all causes of action stated by cross-complainants Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC. As to the causes of action stated by the Martin Building Company, the motion is granted as to the causes of action for equitable indemnity, contribution, and negligence and is denied as to the causes of action for express indemnity and breach of contract; it is moot as to the (dismissed) cause of action for breach of warranty. Rainbow is entitled to summary adjudication of the causes of action for equitable indemnity/ implied contractual indemnity, contribution, and negligence stated by all cross-complainants because these claims are extinguished by the Court's June 5, 2019 order granting a determination of a good faith settlement between Rainbow and Plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association. (Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(c).) The negligence cause of action is extinguished because the negligence cause of action is a disguised claim for equitable indemnity. (See Gackstetter v. Frawley (2006) 135 Cal.App.4th 1257, 1274 ["a party may not avoid a section 877.6 motion by providing different labels for what are in reality indemnity or contribution claims."]; The Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Construction Management, Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595, 607 [agreeing that project architect's negligence claim against construction managers "is essentially a claim for indemnification and an attempt to avoid the consequences of the good faith settlement. To permit [architect's] claim would subvert California's public policy of encouraging good faith settlement."). Summary adjudication is granted on the express indemnity and breach of contract causes of action stated by the McNerney LLC's because the undisputed facts show that there was no contract between the parties. Triable issues of fact preclude summary adjudication on MBC's causes of action for express contractual indemnity and breach of contract. Although Rainbow did not execute MBC's proposed Subcontract, there are triable issues of material fact regarding the terms of the contract between MBC and Rainbow. A trier of fact could find that Rainbow accepted the Subcontract through its conduct based on evidence that: no price was specified in the Letter of Intent; payment was made according to the terms and schedule in the Subcontract; Rainbow added MBC as an additional insured as required by the Subcontract; the change orders referred to the existing "Original Construction Agreement" and "Original Contract Amount"; and Rainbow's rejection of the subcontract was not communicated to MBC (McNerney Decl. 5, 7-9; Andreolli Decl. 5; Abell Decl. Exh. D). (Civ. Code §§ 1584, 1589). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DENIS HARPER ET AL VS. WIEGEL LAW GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Impose Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2019, line 2. DEFENDANT REUBEN, JUNIUS & ROSE'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Impose Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion is unopposed. Plaintiff served verified discovery responses following receipt of the motion. Defendants request the motion be heard because responses include privilege and work product objections and for other reasons. The Court in its discretion agrees to do so. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pac. Healthcare Consultants, 148 Cal. App. 4th 390 (2007). Within twenty days from the date of service of this order with notice of entry plaintiff shall serve code complaint responses to discovery which is the subject of this motion. Plaintiff may file a motion for relief from waiver of objections within the aforesaid twenty days which will temporarily stay service of its responses. Monetary sanctions are imposed against plaintiff's counsel in the sum of $1060.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LINLEY VS. DENISE BARTLETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ashley Kleins Deposition; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Adriana Leiva And Her Attorney Of Record, Andrew Werner, In The Amount Of $1,029.50
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2019, line 3. PLAINTIFF DANIEL LINLEY'S Motion To Compel Ashley Kleins Deposition; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Adriana Leiva And Her Attorney Of Record, Andrew Werner, In The Amount Of $1,029.50. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. An attorney-client relationship existed between the defendants and attorney Ashley Klein. Defendants' affirmative defenses 14, 19, 23 and 30 infer but do not specifically allege "advice of counsel". If "advice of counsel" was specifically plead the result would be waiver of the attorney-client privilege and with discovery permitted. At the hearing the defendants to clarify whether Ms. Klein's will herself be a trial witness and or her specific advice will be otherwise factually proffered. All parties' requests for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Actions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFFsSTANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC, CAIXA CAPITAL RISC, MAGIC STONE ALTERNATIVE INVESTMENTS, REGENT CAPITAL VENTURE LTD., STARTCAPS VENTURES'Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Actions Plaintiffs and Cross-Defendants Stanford-Startx Fund, et al.'s motion to transfer and consolidate actions is granted. The claims asserted by Defendant and Cross-Complainant Arturo Devesa in Devesa v. Stanford University, Santa Clara County Superior Court Case No. 19-CV-347760, share numerous common factual and legal issues with those asserted by Mr. Devesa in his second amended cross-complaint and proposed third amended cross-complaint in this action, and involve many of the same named parties. Mr. Devesa's contention in his late-filed opposition brief that the two actions are "not related" is meritless. Although Mr. Devesa has named additional individual defendants in the Santa Clara action, the underlying allegations and claims overlap substantially. Plaintiffs and Cross-Defendants have shown, and the Court finds, that the cases meet the standards specified in Section 404.1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, are not complex as defined by the Judicial Council, and that the moving parties have made a good faith effort to obtain agreement to the transfer from all parties to each action. Accordingly, pursuant to Section 403 of the Code of Civil Procedure, that action is ordered transferred to this Court, and the two actions are ordered consolidated for all purposes including trial. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 403, 1048.) The instant action shall be the lead action, and all further pleadings shall be filed under this case number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiffs Dental Force, Inc. And Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, Pc To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Number One, Number 16, 18, 19, And 20 And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $4,015.00 From Defendant Bemdad Behnood; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Graham C. Mills In Support Thereof; Separate Statement In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 25, 2019, line 5. PLAINTIFFS DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Number One, Number 16, 18, 19, And 20 And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $4,015.00 From Defendant Bemdad Behnood. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Pursuant to Local Rule 8.2 (A)(3) the parties shall appear in person at the hearing. The Court intends to hear argument and rule on Defendant's objections interposed to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. rulings.Case Title: SQUAR MILNER LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. FRANCK LECLERC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT FRANCK LECLERC, CAFE CLAUDE RESTAURANT INC A CALIFORNIA's Motion To Set Aside Default Defendants Franck LeClerc and Café Claude Restaurant, Inc.'s motion to set aside entry of default is denied. Mr. LeClerc does not dispute that he was properly served with the summons and complaint, and he admits he received the March 6, 2019 letter from Plaintiff's counsel notifying him that Plaintiff would seek entry of default unless he responded to the complaint. Mr. LeClerc contends that he did not understand that he was required to respond to the complaint because the package also included a notice of an initial case management conference, but he does not explain why he (apparently) did not read the summons. Mr. LeClerc also offers no explanation for neglecting to forward the summons and complaint, which he acknowledges he recognized as legal papers, to his corporate counsel. Further, although he acknowledges receiving and signing for the letter sent to him by Plaintiff's counsel with return receipt requested, he asserts that he must have misplaced it without reading it. On this record, Defendants have failed to carry their burden of demonstrating "a satisfactory excuse for not responding to the original complaint in a timely manner." (Stiles v. Wallis (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1143, 1148-1149 [defendant did not show excusable neglect, where the notice on the face of the summons "was calculated to inform anyone capable of reading English . . . of the necessity of filing an answer with the court"]; see Jackson v. Bank of America (1983) 141 Cal.App.3d 55, 58 ["the acts which brought about the default must have been the acts of a reasonably prudent person under the same circumstances"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS, INC. VS. MID CITY CANNABIS CLUB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 25, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ADVANCED PUBLIC AWARENESS, INC.'s Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment Plaintiff Center for Advanced Public Awareness, Inc.'s motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warnings that are required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order and judgment included in the moving papers. =(302/EPS)
